description: Client environment overview — at-a-glance health, active alerts, VM performance hot spots, and monitoring coverage. Built so you never need to leave this page.

panels.description: How many VMs sent a heartbeat in the last hour. If this number is lower than your total VMs, something is offline or its agent stopped working.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
Heartbeat
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1h)
| summarize MachinesReporting = dcount(Computer)

panels.title: Reporting VMs

panels.description: Minutes since ANY machine last checked in. Green means data is flowing. Yellow/red means monitoring may be broken — investigate immediately.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
Heartbeat
| summarize LastHeartbeat = max(TimeGenerated)
| extend MinutesSinceHeartbeat = datetime_diff('minute', now(), LastHeartbeat)
| project MinutesSinceHeartbeat

panels.title: Heartbeat Freshness

panels.description: Total VMs in this subscription. Compare against Reporting VMs to spot monitoring gaps.

azureResourceGraph.query:
Resources
| where subscriptionId == ${sub:singlequote}
| where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines'
| summarize VMCount = count()

panels.title: Total VMs

panels.description: VMs that exist but did NOT send a heartbeat in the last hour. Zero is the goal. Any number above zero means you have blind spots.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
Heartbeat
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1h)
| distinct Computer
| summarize Reporting = count()

azureResourceGraph.query:
Resources
| where subscriptionId == ${sub:singlequote}
| where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines'
| summarize TotalVMs = count()

panels.title: VMs Not Reporting

panels.description: VMs that exist in the subscription but have NOT sent a heartbeat in the last hour. These are your blind spots — either offline, deallocated, or missing a monitoring agent.

azureResourceGraph.query:
Resources
| where subscriptionId == ${sub:singlequote}
| where type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines'
| extend powerState = tostring(properties.extended.instanceView.powerState.code)
| extend vmSize = tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize)
| project VMName = name, ResourceGroup = resourceGroup, Region = location, Size = vmSize, PowerState = case(
    powerState == 'PowerState/running', 'Running',
    powerState == 'PowerState/deallocated', 'Deallocated',
    powerState == 'PowerState/stopped', 'Stopped',
    coalesce(powerState, 'Unknown')
)
| order by ResourceGroup asc, VMName asc

panels.title: VM Inventory & Power State

panels.description: Active Sev0/Sev1 alerts — production-impacting issues that need immediate human attention. If this is red, stop what you're doing and investigate.

azureResourceGraph.query:
AlertsManagementResources
| where subscriptionId in~ (${sub:singlequote})
| where properties.essentials.monitorCondition =~ "Fired"
| where tostring(properties.essentials.severity) in ("Sev0","Sev1")
| summarize CriticalAlerts = count()

panels.title: Critical Alerts

panels.description: Active Sev2–Sev4 alerts — degraded conditions or early warning signs. Not emergencies, but don't let these pile up.

azureResourceGraph.query:
AlertsManagementResources
| where subscriptionId in~ (${sub:singlequote})
| where properties.essentials.monitorCondition =~ "Fired"
| where tostring(properties.essentials.severity) in ("Sev2","Sev3","Sev4")
| summarize WarningAlerts = dcount(id)

panels.title: Warning Alerts

panels.description: How long the oldest active alert has been firing. If this keeps growing, alerts are being ignored. A healthy team keeps this under a few hours.

azureResourceGraph.query:
AlertsManagementResources
| where subscriptionId in~ (${sub:singlequote})
| where properties.essentials.monitorCondition =~ "Fired"
| extend StartTime = todatetime(properties.essentials.startDateTime)
| summarize OldestMinutes = datetime_diff('minute', now(), min(StartTime))
| project OldestMinutes = iff(isnull(OldestMinutes), 0, OldestMinutes)

panels.title: Oldest Open Alert

panels.description: Alerts resolved in the last 24 hours. Shows the team is actively closing issues. If Critical + Warning are high but this is zero, nobody is responding.

azureResourceGraph.query:
AlertsManagementResources
| where subscriptionId in~ (${sub:singlequote})
| where properties.essentials.monitorCondition =~ "Resolved"
| where todatetime(properties.essentials.lastModifiedDateTime) >= ago(24h)
| summarize ResolvedAlerts = count()

panels.title: Resolved (24h)

panels.description: All currently firing alerts. Sorted by severity so the worst problems are at the top. Use this to triage — check Severity, Resource, and Time Open first.

azureResourceGraph.query:
AlertsManagementResources
| where subscriptionId in~ (${sub:singlequote})
| where properties.essentials.monitorCondition =~ "Fired"
| where tostring(properties.essentials.severity) in ("Sev0","Sev1","Sev2","Sev3","Sev4")
| extend Severity = tostring(properties.essentials.severity)
| extend SeverityLabel = case(
    Severity == "Sev0", "Critical",
    Severity == "Sev1", "Error",
    Severity == "Sev2", "Warning",
    Severity == "Sev3", "Info",
    Severity == "Sev4", "Verbose",
    Severity
)
| extend
    TargetResourceType = tostring(properties.essentials.targetResourceType),
    TargetResourceName = tostring(properties.essentials.targetResourceName),
    MonitorService = tostring(properties.essentials.monitorService)
| extend Category = case(
    tolower(TargetResourceType) contains "virtualmachines", "Virtual Machine",
    tolower(MonitorService) contains "servicehealth", "Service Health",
    tolower(TargetResourceType) contains "subscriptions", "Subscription",
    isempty(TargetResourceType) and tolower(MonitorService) contains "servicehealth", "Service Health",
    "Other"
)
| extend StartTime = todatetime(properties.essentials.startDateTime)
| extend TimeOpenMin = datetime_diff('minute', now(), StartTime)
| extend TimeOpen = case(
    TimeOpenMin < 60, strcat(tostring(TimeOpenMin), 'm'),
    TimeOpenMin < 1440, strcat(tostring(TimeOpenMin / 60), 'h ', tostring(TimeOpenMin % 60), 'm'),
    strcat(tostring(TimeOpenMin / 1440), 'd ', tostring((TimeOpenMin % 1440) / 60), 'h')
)
| project
    SeverityLabel,
    Severity,
    Category,
    Resource = TargetResourceName,
    Reason = tostring(properties.essentials.description),
    ResourceGroup = tostring(properties.essentials.targetResourceGroup),
    TimeOpen,
    StartTime
| order by Severity asc, StartTime asc

panels.title: Active Alerts

panels.description: Average CPU across all monitored VMs. Sustained yellow/orange means workloads are pushing capacity. Note: this is an average — a single overloaded VM can hide behind healthy neighbors. Check 'Top CPU' below for per-VM breakdown.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
Perf
| where CounterName == "% Processor Time"
| where InstanceName == "_Total" or ObjectName == "Processor"
| summarize AvgCPU = avg(CounterValue) by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: CPU %

panels.description: Average memory usage across all VMs. Memory pressure above 85% often leads to swapping and performance degradation. Check 'Top Memory' below for which specific VMs are running hot.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
Perf
| where CounterName == "% Committed Bytes In Use"
| summarize AvgMemory = avg(CounterValue) by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Memory %

panels.description: Average disk space used across all VMs. Disks filling past 90% cause application failures, log rotation issues, and potential data loss. Check 'Top Disk' below for individual VMs at risk.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
Perf
| where ObjectName == "LogicalDisk"
| where CounterName == "% Free Space"
| extend UsedPct = 100.0 - CounterValue
| summarize DiskUsedPct = avg(UsedPct) by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Disk Used %

panels.description: Average network throughput across all monitored machines. Useful for spotting unusual traffic patterns — a sudden spike could indicate a backup job, data migration, or something unexpected.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
Perf
| where CounterName == "Bytes Total/sec"
| summarize NetworkMBps = avg(CounterValue) / 1024 / 1024 by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Network Throughput

panels.description: Volume of heartbeat signals over time. A steady line means machines are healthy and checking in. A sudden drop means one or more machines went dark — cross-reference with 'Reporting VMs' above.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
Heartbeat
| summarize Heartbeats = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Heartbeat Timeline

panels.description: Error-level system events over time. Spikes here often correlate with service failures, unexpected reboots, or application crashes. Quiet is good.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
Event
| where EventLevelName == "Error"
| summarize Errors = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Error Events

panels.description: Volume of performance metrics being collected. A steady flow means monitoring is healthy. If this drops to zero, data collection is broken and every other panel on this dashboard goes blind.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
InsightsMetrics
| summarize Metrics = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Monitoring Data Flow

panels.description: The 10 VMs consuming the most CPU. Red means the machine is near capacity and may need to be scaled up or have its workload redistributed.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let startDateTime = $__timeFrom;
let endDateTime = $__timeTo;
Perf
| where TimeGenerated between (startDateTime .. endDateTime)
| where CounterName == "% Processor Time"
| where InstanceName == "_Total"
| extend ComputerId = iff(isempty(_ResourceId), Computer, _ResourceId)
| summarize AvgCPU = round(avg(CounterValue), 1) by Computer
| top 10 by AvgCPU desc

panels.title: Top CPU

panels.description: The 10 VMs with highest memory usage. VMs consistently above 85% are candidates for upsizing or workload optimization.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let startDateTime = $__timeFrom;
let endDateTime = $__timeTo;
Perf
| where TimeGenerated between (startDateTime .. endDateTime)
| where CounterName == "% Committed Bytes In Use"
| summarize AvgMemory = round(avg(CounterValue), 1) by Computer
| top 10 by AvgMemory desc

panels.title: Top Memory

panels.description: The 10 VMs with the least free disk space. Anything above 90% is a ticking time bomb — logs stop, apps crash, databases corrupt.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let startDateTime = $__timeFrom;
let endDateTime = $__timeTo;
Perf
| where TimeGenerated between (startDateTime .. endDateTime)
| where ObjectName == "LogicalDisk"
| where CounterName == "% Free Space"
| extend UsedPct = 100.0 - CounterValue
| summarize AvgDisk = round(avg(UsedPct), 1) by Computer
| top 10 by AvgDisk desc

panels.title: Top Disk

panels.description: The 10 VMs pushing the most network traffic. Useful for identifying data-heavy workloads or unexpected bandwidth consumers.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let startDateTime = $__timeFrom;
let endDateTime = $__timeTo;
Perf
| where TimeGenerated between (startDateTime .. endDateTime)
| where CounterName == "Bytes Total/sec"
| summarize AvgNet = round(avg(CounterValue) / 1024 / 1024, 2) by Computer
| top 10 by AvgNet desc

panels.title: Top Network

panels.description: Per-VM CPU stats with percentile breakdown. P95 is the most important column — it tells you what the VM is doing 95% of the time, filtering out brief spikes. If P95 is high, the machine genuinely needs more CPU.

azureResourceGraph.query:
resources
| where tolower(type) contains "virtualmachines" and resourceGroup in~ ($rg) 
| project Name = tolower(name), tenantId, resourceGroup

panels.title: Top 10 CPU Utilization (Percentile Breakdown)

panels.description: Per-VM memory stats. High P95 memory with a much higher Max suggests occasional memory pressure spikes. Consistently high across all percentiles means the VM is undersized for its workload.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let startDateTime = $__timeFrom;
let endDateTime = $__timeTo;
let maxResultCount = 10;
Perf
| where TimeGenerated between (startDateTime .. endDateTime)
| where CounterName == "% Committed Bytes In Use"
| extend ComputerId = iff(isempty(_ResourceId), Computer, _ResourceId)
| summarize Average = round(avg(CounterValue), 2), Max = round(max(CounterValue), 2), P50th = round(percentile(CounterValue, 50), 2), P90th = round(percentile(CounterValue, 90), 2), P95th = round(percentile(CounterValue, 95), 2) by ComputerId, Computer, _ResourceId
| top maxResultCount by Average desc
| parse tolower(_ResourceId) with * "resourcegroups/" resourceGroup "/providers/microsoft.compute/" typeVM "/" nameVM
| project Computer, resourceGroup, Average, P50th, P90th, P95th, Max, Type = typeVM, Name = nameVM

azureResourceGraph.query:
resources
| where tolower(type) contains "virtualmachines" and resourceGroup in~ ($rg) 
| project Name = tolower(name), tenantId, resourceGroup

panels.title: Top 10 Memory Utilization (Percentile Breakdown)

panels.description: Per-VM disk utilization including mount point. The MountId column shows which specific drive is filling up — critical for knowing whether it's the OS disk or a data volume.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let startDateTime = $__timeFrom;
let endDateTime = $__timeTo;
let maxResultCount = 10;
InsightsMetrics
| where TimeGenerated between (startDateTime .. endDateTime)
| where Origin == 'vm.azm.ms' and (Namespace == 'LogicalDisk' and Name == 'FreeSpaceMB')
| extend ComputerId = iff(isempty(_ResourceId), Computer, _ResourceId)
| extend Tags = todynamic(Tags)
| extend Total = todouble(Tags['vm.azm.ms/diskSizeMB']), MountId = tostring(Tags['vm.azm.ms/mountId'])
| extend Val = (100.0 - (Val * 100.0)/Total)
| summarize Average = round(avg(Val), 2), Max = round(max(Val), 2), P50th = round(percentile(Val, 50), 2), P90th = round(percentile(Val, 90), 2), P95th = round(percentile(Val, 95), 2) by MountId, ComputerId, Computer, _ResourceId
| top maxResultCount by Average desc
| parse tolower(_ResourceId) with * "resourcegroups/" resourceGroup "/providers/microsoft.compute/" typeVM "/" nameVM
| project Computer, resourceGroup, MountId, Average, P50th, P90th, P95th, Max, Type = typeVM, Name = nameVM

azureResourceGraph.query:
resources
| where tolower(type) contains "virtualmachines" and resourceGroup in~ ($rg) 
| project Name = tolower(name), tenantId, resourceGroup

panels.title: Top 10 Disk Space Usage (Percentile Breakdown)

panels.description: Per-VM inbound network throughput in MB/s. High P95 values identify VMs that consistently pull significant data — useful for capacity planning and bandwidth cost analysis.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let startDateTime = $__timeFrom;
let endDateTime = $__timeTo;
let maxResultCount = 10;
InsightsMetrics
| where TimeGenerated between (startDateTime .. endDateTime)
| where Origin == 'vm.azm.ms' and (Namespace == 'Network' and Name == 'ReadBytesPerSecond')
| extend ComputerId = iff(isempty(_ResourceId), Computer, _ResourceId)
| summarize Average = round(avg(Val)/1024/1024, 3), Max = round(max(Val)/1024/1024, 3), P50th = round(percentile(Val, 50)/1024/1024, 3), P90th = round(percentile(Val, 90)/1024/1024, 3), P95th = round(percentile(Val, 95)/1024/1024, 3) by ComputerId, Computer, _ResourceId
| top maxResultCount by Average desc
| parse tolower(_ResourceId) with * "resourcegroups/" resourceGroup "/providers/microsoft.compute/" typeVM "/" nameVM
| project Computer, resourceGroup, Average, P50th, P90th, P95th, Max, Type = typeVM, Name = nameVM

azureResourceGraph.query:
resources
| where tolower(type) contains "virtualmachines" and resourceGroup in~ ($rg) 
| project Name = tolower(name), tenantId, resourceGroup

panels.title: Top 10 Network Receive (Percentile Breakdown)

panels.description: Every Azure resource in this subscription. Use the column headers to sort and the search bar to filter. The 'Type' column shows human-friendly resource categories.

azureResourceGraph.query:
Resources
| where subscriptionId == ${sub:singlequote}
| extend FriendlyType = case(
    type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines', 'Virtual Machine',
    type =~ 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets', 'VM Scale Set',
    type =~ 'microsoft.storage/storageaccounts', 'Storage Account',
    type =~ 'microsoft.network/virtualnetworks', 'Virtual Network',
    type =~ 'microsoft.network/networksecuritygroups', 'NSG',
    type =~ 'microsoft.network/publicipaddresses', 'Public IP',
    type =~ 'microsoft.network/networkinterfaces', 'Network Interface',
    type =~ 'microsoft.network/loadbalancers', 'Load Balancer',
    type =~ 'microsoft.network/applicationgateways', 'App Gateway',
    type =~ 'microsoft.web/sites', 'App Service',
    type =~ 'microsoft.web/serverfarms', 'App Service Plan',
    type =~ 'microsoft.sql/servers', 'SQL Server',
    type =~ 'microsoft.sql/servers/databases', 'SQL Database',
    type =~ 'microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters', 'AKS Cluster',
    type =~ 'microsoft.keyvault/vaults', 'Key Vault',
    type =~ 'microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces', 'Log Analytics Workspace',
    type =~ 'microsoft.compute/disks', 'Managed Disk',
    type =~ 'microsoft.network/privatednszones', 'Private DNS Zone',
    type =~ 'microsoft.recoveryservices/vaults', 'Recovery Vault',
    type =~ 'microsoft.automation/automationaccounts', 'Automation Account',
    type
)
| project
    Name = name,
    Type = FriendlyType,
    ResourceGroup = resourceGroup,
    Region = location
| order by Type asc, ResourceGroup asc, Name asc

panels.title: All Resources

title: Client Overview
